source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A triangular piece of paper is folded along the dotted line shown in the left-hand diagram to form the heptagon shown in the right-hand diagram. The total area of the shaded parts of the heptagon is $1 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$. The area of the original triangle is $1 \frac{1}{2}$ times the area of the heptagon. What is the area of the original triangle, in $\mathrm{cm}^{2}$ ? <image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly references a left-hand diagram, right-hand diagram, and a dotted fold line
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual is essential rather than optional
the task depends on geometric folding, shaded regions, and resulting shape structure, which are visual/spatial properties
multi_step: solving appears to require interpreting the fold diagram and relating the shaded parts to area changes after folding
the problem combines at least two conditions: shaded-area information and the ratio between original triangle area and heptagon area
this is a geometry word problem with image-grounded reasoning plus numeric area computation, suggesting multi-step multimodal reasoning
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ('3'), which is easy to check against a derived result
the problem gives explicit quantitative constraints (shaded area equals 1 cm^2 and original triangle area is 1.5 times the heptagon area)
asset_registry_record shows question and answer are present and registry_passed=true, so the sample is structurally suitable for verification

risk_flags: diagram-specific geometry details are required for full solution verification

decision_reasons: question text, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are well supported
